An olfactory receptor cell in which the apical ending of the dendrite is a knob that bears numerous microvilli. The cell body is plump and the soma is at an  intermediate position in the olfactory epithelium.

An olfactory receptor cell with short cilia growing in an invagination bordered by microvilli.  The soma is large and globose located in the apical region of the olfactory epithelium.  The axons project to a single glomerulus mdG2.
A neuron that releases serotonin as a neurotransmitter.

comments: Has alternate ID CL:0000403.

definition.val: Neuromast mantle cell is a non-sensory cell. Neuromast mantle cells surround the neuromast support cells and neuromast hair cells, separating the neuromast from the epidermis, and secrete cupula in which the ciliary bundles of all the hair cells are embedded.

Neuromast support cell is a non-sensory cell of the neuromast that extend between the sensory hair cells from the basement membrane to the apical surface; neuromast support cells are surrounded by neuromast mantle cells.
Olfactory epithelial support cell is a non ciliated columnar cell that extends from the epithelial free margin to the basement membrane of the olfactory epithelium.
Interneuromast cell is a neuroectodermal cell deposited by the migrating lateral line primordium between the neuromasts. Interneuromast cells proliferate and migrate to form additional neuromasts.
Hair cell is a mechanoreceptor cell that is sensitive to movement of the hair-like projections (stereocilia and kinocilia) which relay the information centrally in the nervous system.

Neuromast hair cell is a hair cell that acts as a sensory receptor of the neuromast; it is morphologically polarized as a result of the relative position of the single kinocilium and the clusters of stereocilia on its apical surface.
A skeletal muscle myoblast that differentiates into slow muscle fibers.
A skeletal muscle myoblast that differentiates into fast muscle fibers.
Epithelial cell part of duct.

comments: Definition not present in CL.

definition.val: An epithelial cell of the kidney.
A cuboidal epithelial cell of the kidney that regulates acid/base balance.
A cuboidal epithelial cell of the kidney which secretes acid and reabsorbs base to regulate acid/base balance.
An epithelial cell of the intrahepatic portion of the bile duct.
Epithelial cell found in the ducts of the pancreas.
An epithelial cell derived from endoderm.
Epithelial cells derived from neural plate and neural crest.

comments: The term "neuroepithelial cell" is used to describe both this cell type and sensory epithelial cell (CL:0000098).

definition.val: An epithelial cell derived from ectoderm.
A cell of an organism that does not pass on its genetic material to the organism's offspring (i.e. a non-germ line cell).
A cell whose primary function is to support other cell types.
Epithelial cell derived from mesoderm or mesenchyme.
Cell that is part of the kidney.
A cell of the supporting or framework tissue of the body, arising chiefly from the embryonic mesoderm and including adipose tissue, cartilage, and bone.
Lymphatic progenitor cells.

comments: Usually express Prox1, or prox1b.

definition.val: Mesenchymal derived lymphatic progenitor cells that give rise to the superficial lymphatics.
Lymphatic progenitor cells, derived from the veins, that give rise to lymphatic endothelial cells.
The neurons that utilize glycine as a neurotransmitter.

An epithelial cell that is part of the bile duct. Cholangiocytes contribute to bile secretion via net release of bicarbonate and water. They are cuboidal epithelium in the small interlobular bile ducts, but become columnar and mucus secreting in larger bile ducts approaching the porta hepatis and the extrahepatic ducts.
Multi fate stem cell that gives rise to both hepatocytes and cholangiocytes as descendants.
An epithelial cell of the intestine.
An epithelial cell of the esophagus.
A  vertebrate lens cell that is any of the elongated, tightly packed cells that make up the bulk of the mature lens in a camera-type eye.
Cell that is part of the heart valve.
Cells that secrete the extracellular matrix of the heart valve.
Endothelial cells that surround the heart valve extracellular matrix.
